summary: Generated and wired a Route Replay chronometer texture so the Path Map replay controller has a premium game-like timeline device backdrop and a discoverable Visual Asset Vault record.

queries: game UI timeline replay controls quest log visual design
interactive timeline playback dashboard UI design pattern
visual asset vault design system dashboard game UI inspiration
pattern_notes: Timeline interfaces benefit from a clear axis, playhead, and visible stage points.
Game UI relies on visual cues that guide the intended action without overexplaining.
Generated system assets should be visible in the same vault as lane, game, bot, and trophy art so forks can audit what powers each surface.

image_generation.prompt_summary: Premium sci-fi fantasy route chronometer artwork with a glowing horizontal timeline, playhead crystal, time rings, waypoint sparks, dark graphite glass, teal/amber/green glow, no text, no logos, and UI-safe darker edges.

ui_contract: Use the generated chronometer image as a subdued Route Replay backdrop without reducing text readability.
Keep Route Replay controls above the image layer and responsive at mobile widths.
Register the generated image as a system asset in Visual Asset Vault.
Document the asset path so standalone forks can replace or extend it.

image_file.evidence: route-replay-chronometer-20260618.png is a valid 1672x941 PNG copied into web/assets/system.

static.evidence: node --check web/app.js, snapshot JSON validation, edited-file ASCII checks, and HTTP 200 for the Path Map route all passed.

browser_path_map.evidence: Route Replay rendered with CSS background-image containing route-replay-chronometer-20260618.png, before-opacity 0.42, Stage 1 of 64, no bad rectangles, and no console errors.

browser_asset_vault.evidence: After selecting the system Asset Vault filter, the Route Replay chronometer card rendered with image src ./assets/system/route-replay-chronometer-20260618.png at 317x154 and no bad rectangles.

asset_http.evidence: Asset served over the local app with HTTP 200, image/png, and 1976314 bytes.
